items.reason: decisions-needed.md lists 20 decision areas (DEC-001 through DEC-020) and standards-overlap-decisions.md contains decisions with id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to populated for each. Sampled DEC-001, DEC-016, DEC-019, DEC-020 all have non-empty projects_to lists pointing to dictionary fields, migrations, and code paths.
decisions-pending.md contains entries with owner (Andy), target dates (2026-05-15, 2026-05-03), blocker, and rationale. Both target dates are in the future relative to the loop's research date.

Sampled DEC-001 through DEC-020 consequences sections each name concrete artifacts removed/eliminated (e.g., DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts...', DEC-007 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table', DEC-016 'Removes direct edits to dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json...'). Each also includes 'Why not collapse' justifications against more aggressive alternatives.

Sampled decisions include three credible options. DEC-005 considers AGS-as-canonical, peer-source-native stores, and the chosen layered approach. DEC-009 considers QTI-as-canonical-content, peer cartridges/QTI/LTI/Caliper, and a broad resource concept. These are real architectural choices production platforms make, not strawmen.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json contains projection_policy citing DEC-016 with scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py as generator and --check drift gate. The seed shows projection_summary with 5 specs, 59 objects, 575 fields. VERIFY.md runs `python3 scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py --check` and the script enforces drift detection per check_projection_drift().

data/data-dictionary.seed.json shows canonical.identity.person.email with projections to oneroster-core, caliper-core, integration-governance-core, and qti-core fields. The canonical_objects array enumerates shared fields with bidirectional projection lists, satisfying the email-across-four-specs example.

canonical.identity.person object exists in data/data-dictionary.seed.json with its own fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed values, and explicit projections to OneRoster Person, Caliper Actor, QTI Candidate, and LTI membership/launch. spec_only_projections documents QTI candidate as deferred per DEC-012.

Per-spec dictionaries (oneroster-core.v1.json, caliper-core.v1.json, case-core.v1.json, qti-core.v1.json, integration-governance-core.v1.json) contain shared_enums arrays with canonical_values, crosswalk, and reverse_crosswalk. Example: grading_progress in oneroster-core.v1.json maps both OneRoster score_status (notSubmitted, submitted, partiallyGraded, fullyGraded) and LTI grading_progress (FullyGraded, Pending, PendingManual, Failed, NotReady) bidirectionally to canonical values.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json privacy_classes contains exactly the closed list (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system) and scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py validate_privacy_classes() enforces equality with CLOSED_PRIVACY_CLASSES and rejects depends_on_* placeholders. Each class cites DEC-019.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json migration_generation block cites DEC-020 with scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py as generator. The seed declares 13 relationship_keys mapped to objects[].relationships arrays. VERIFY.md runs `python3 scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py --check` and the generator produces supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql with FK clauses derived from the graph.

Sampled fields, objects, enums, and privacy classes carry decision_id (e.g., privacy_classes cite DEC-019, shared_enums cite DEC-018, canonical fields cite DEC-001/007). scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py validates decision_id references resolve to decisions in standards-overlap-decisions.md via the decision-trace block, and per-field traces are enforced.

scripts/generate_spec_fidelity_report.py exists and enforces the seam: copy-exact JSON keys for 1EdTech-native fields, snake_case column derivation, extension_rationale required. VERIFY.md runs both --report-only and --check. The script exits non-zero on silent rename or missing rationale, and renders docs/spec-fidelity-report.md with per-spec breakdown, defect section, and extension table.

Per-spec generators (generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_qti_core.py, generate_case_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py, generate_integration_governance_core.py) emit Markdown and HTML pages from each dictionary source. scripts/check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies every object/field/value/decision_id appears in generated SQL, OpenAPI, Markdown, and HTML, failing on drift. VERIFY.md runs all generators plus the artifact check.

docs/lead-spec-accounting.md includes a Decision Citation Map mapping each Lead area to its decision_id with plain-language explanation of the simplification (e.g., 'DEC-005-results-scores: Gradebook line items and results use a stable result contract while richer score-scale/profile fields stay deferred'). Generated decision page exists at site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html with per-decision anchors verified by check_site_links.py.

docs/build-an-edtech-app.md walks through all five required steps: (1) creates org_build_school plus 11 people, (2) creates session/course/class plus 11 enrollments, (3) posts numeric grade with line_item and result, (4) PATCHes line_items.case_target_uri to a CASE URI, (5) inserts a Caliper GradeEvent and reads class_activity_feed. Every step includes copy-pasteable curl against live Supabase REST and links each field/enum value to the dictionary anchor (e.g., person.email, role_family.teacher).

site/api/site-link-check.json reports status=pass with 0 errors, 38 reachable HTML pages out of 38, 230 local links checked, 634 dictionary entries verified, 20 decisions reachable, 12 API endpoints checked, all within max_clicks=3. scripts/check_site_links.py is run by VERIFY.md and validates anchors, dictionary reachability, decision anchors, and buildability guide reachability.

The buildability guide is comprehensive and copy-pasteable, but a layperson reading only docs/ encounters one ambiguity in step 4: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md links case_item.uri but the guide PATCHes line_items.case_target_uri with a fabricated `https://standards.example.test/...` URI without any docs-only path to discover real CASE framework URIs (CASE import is deferred per DEC-012, PEND-001). The doc references case_item.uri in the dictionary but the layperson cannot discover or validate a real CASE URI because no CASE framework is loaded in the runtime — they must invent a URI. This is a known partial slice acknowledged in DEC-012 but not flagged as a docs gap in the guide itself.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md step 4: add a one-line explainer noting that CASE framework import is deferred (link to DEC-012 and PEND-001) and that any URI string is acceptable in the current sandbox; without this the layperson does not know whether the URI must resolve.

items.reason: This evaluator output includes traced_cause on every non-pass item naming the specific artifact and required change (e.g., the buildable_by_layperson partial above traces to a specific edit in docs/build-an-edtech-app.md step 4). The evaluator schema enforces non-empty traced_cause for non-pass status.

VERIFY.md final command is `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --runs 3 --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. site/api/platform-evaluation.json exists. scripts/codex_loop.py load_verify_evaluator_output() requires run_count >= 3 and writes the consensus to the file.

scripts/codex_loop.py read_overrides() reads docs/loop-overrides.md, overrides_paused() checks for PAUSE token on its own line outside HTML comments before each iteration, and build_prompt() injects overrides under 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' section. The pause check runs before iteration starts.

scripts/codex_loop.py _run_loop() reads after_eval from VERIFY-stage load_verify_evaluator_output() and terminates only when after_eval.get('done') is true. When Codex narrative says LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE but evaluator says done=false, the loop logs 'Codex reported COMPLETE but evaluator says work remains. Continuing.' and continues iterating.

scripts/codex_loop.py maybe_publish() runs `git fetch origin main` then `git pull --rebase origin main` before `git push origin main`. On rebase failure, it captures conflict paths via `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U`, runs `git rebase --abort`, returns a 'DIVERGENCE:' result that _run_loop() detects and returns 1 to halt scheduling. PROGRESS.md captures the divergence string per append_machine_progress().

VERIFY.md runs `--runs 3`. scripts/codex_loop.py load_verify_evaluator_output() raises EvaluatorError if run_count < 3. format_evaluator_runs_for_progress() records every run's per-item statuses and disagreements in PROGRESS.md. The evaluator references determinism.runs and determinism.consensus_rule in the report. The --runs flag is enforced as max(3, args.evaluator_runs).

scripts/codex_loop.py append_machine_progress() takes verify_counts and verify_done from after_eval (the VERIFY-stage report), not from Codex's last_message. When LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE appears in final_text but after_eval done=false, append_machine_progress() writes a 'narrative_verify_mismatch:' line with both claims. The mismatch does not terminate the loop (controlled by after_eval.get('done') in _run_loop).

scripts/codex_loop.py maybe_publish() calls staged_eval_matches_verify() which reads the staged site/api/platform-evaluation.json via `git show :site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, compares via comparable_eval() (which strips evaluatedAt), and returns False with summarize_eval_diff() detail on any other difference. The publish path returns 'skipped: staged evaluation does not match VERIFY output' on mismatch, blocking the commit.

buildability_gaps.gap: The guide instructs the layperson to PATCH line_items.case_target_uri to a fabricated CASE URI (https://standards.example.test/math/2026/6.NS.A.1) but does not explain that CASE framework import is deferred per DEC-012/PEND-001, leaving the reader uncertain whether the URI must resolve to a real CASE item or whether any string is acceptable in the current sandbox.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md '4. Link The Assignment To A CASE Standard URI': add a one-sentence note before the curl, e.g., 'CASE framework import is deferred (see DEC-012 and PEND-001); the runtime stores the URI as an opaque reference, so any well-formed URI is accepted in the current sandbox.'

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. The teaching-app runtime also backs the narrow CASE alignment path needed by gradebook line items (`line_items.case_target_uri`) and the narrow Caliper class-feed path needed by the buildability guide (`caliper_events` plus `class_activity_feed`). QTI and full CASE import/search remain doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated table, view, Edge Function receipt, or governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, full Sensor API ingestion/projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.

temperature.note: Anthropic Messages API does not expose a seed parameter. For models that still expose temperature, the evaluator pins it to 0. claude-opus-4-7 rejects the temperature parameter as deprecated, so the loop relies on N>=3 safer consensus for that model.

seed.note: Anthropic Messages API does not expose a seed parameter. For models that still expose temperature, the evaluator pins it to 0. claude-opus-4-7 rejects the temperature parameter as deprecated, so the loop relies on N>=3 safer consensus for that model.

determinism.consensus_rule: If all runs agree, keep the unanimous verdict. If any per-item status disagrees, select the safer lower-pass status using fail < partial < blocked < pass. If any run reports done=false, the consensus done flag is false.
